practicals.title: Determination of pH using fruit juices/acid/base/salt solutions
practicals.details: Using pH paper or universal indicator.

practicals.title: Comparing the pH of strong and weak acids
Study pH change in titration using universal indicator
Study the shift in equilibrium between ferric ions and thiocyanate ions
Study the shift in equilibrium between [Co(H2O)6]2+ and chloride ions
Preparation of standard solution of Oxalic acid/Sodium hydroxide/Hydrochloric acid
Determination of strength of Sodium hydroxide by titration against Oxalic acid
Determination of strength of Hydrochloric acid by titration against Sodium Carbonate
Qualitative Analysis (One anion and one cation)
practicals.details: Cations: Pb2+, Cu2+, Al3+, Fe3+, Mn2+, Ni2+, Zn2+, Co2+, Ca2+, Sr2+, Ba2+, Mg2+, NH4+. Anions: CO3 2-, S2-, SO3 2-, NO3 -, NO2 -, Cl-, Br-, I-, SO4 2-, PO4 3-, CH3COO-.

practicals.title: Detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds
Preparation of lyophilic and lyophobic sols (Starch/Egg albumin/Gum / Ferric hydroxide)
Dialysis of sol prepared in Surface Chemistry section
Study the role of emulsifying agents in stabilizing emulsion
Effect of concentration and temperature on rate of reaction (Sodium Thiosulphate + HCl)
Study of reaction rates (Iodide ion with Hydrogen Peroxide / KIO3 and Na2SO3 clock reaction)
Enthalpy of dissolution (Copper Sulphate or Potassium Nitrate) / Enthalpy of neutralization / Hydrogen bond formation study
Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn2+ || Cu2+/Cu with change in concentration
Separation of pigments by paper chromatography and determination of Rf values
Preparation of double salt (Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate or Potash Alum)
Preparation of Potassium Ferric Oxalate
Preparation of Organic Compound (Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, p-Nitroacetanilide, Aniline yellow)
Tests for functional groups (Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic, amino)
Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins in pure samples/foodstuffs

marks_distribution.component: Practical Examination for Visually Challenged Students (Classes XI & XII)

question_paper_design.rule_1: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
question_paper_design.rule_2: Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
question_paper_design.rule_3: There will be no overall choice in the question paper.

question_paper_design.internal_choice_policy: 33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.

competency_weightage.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

internal_assessment.policy: Schools can integrate formative topics with existing chapters as they align well.
formative_topics_class_xii: Surface Chemistry - Adsorption and Colloidal State
General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements

class_xi_mandatory_experiments_section_b: Quantitative analysis (Oxalic acid/KMnO4 or Mohr's salt)
Qualitative Analysis (One anion and one cation)

class_xii_mandatory_experiments_section_: Quantitative analysis (Oxalic acid/KMnO4 or Mohr's salt)
Qualitative Analysis (One anion and one cation)

class_xi_suggested_projects: Checking the bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
Study of the methods of purification of water

Testing the hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride, etc., depending upon the regional variation in drinking water and study of causes of presence of these ions above permissible limit (if any)
Investigation of the foaming capacity of different washing soaps and the effect of addition of Sodium carbonate on it
Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves
Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids
Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers
Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices
class_xii_suggested_projects: Study of the presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening
Study of quantity of casein present in different samples of milk
Preparation of soybean milk and its comparison with the natural milk with respect to curd formation, effect of temperature, etc.
Study of the effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions (temperature, concentration, time, etc.)
Study of digestion of starch by salivary amylase and effect of pH and temperature on it
Comparative study of the rate of fermentation of following materials: wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice, etc.
Extraction of essential oils present in Saunf (aniseed), Ajwain (carom), Illaichi (cardamom)
Study of common food adulterants in fat, oil, butter, sugar, turmeric power, chili powder and pepper

general_requirements.nature: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

long_chapter_names_reclassified: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Foundational understanding of matter, measurement, and quantitative chemical analysis.
competencies: Explain the nature of matter and its properties at macroscopic levels.
Apply laws of chemical combination to predict reaction outcomes.
Perform stoichiometric calculations involving mole concept and molar masses.
Analyze uncertainty in measurement and apply significant figures rules.

competency_groups.title: Structure of Atom
competency_groups.description: Understanding the evolution of atomic models and quantum mechanical principles.
competencies: Trace the historical development from Dalton to Quantum Mechanical Model.
Describe sub-atomic particles and their discovery.
Apply Bohr's model concepts to hydrogen atom spectra.
Differentiate between classical and quantum descriptions of electron behavior.

competency_groups.title: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.description: Mastery of periodic trends, electronic configurations, and block classification.
competencies: Justify the need for element classification based on properties.
Predict elemental properties using modern periodic law and position in table.
Determine electronic configuration and classify elements into s, p, d, f blocks.
Analyze trends in atomic radii, ionization enthalpy, and electronegativity.
competency_groups.unit_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.chapter_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

competency_groups.title: Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure
competency_groups.description: Ability to predict molecular geometry, bond types, and stability using bonding theories.
competencies: Apply Kossel-Lewis approach to explain ionic and covalent bond formation.
Use VSEPR theory to predict molecular shapes based on electron pairs.
Explain hybridization concepts in relation to orbital overlap.
Differentiate between Valence Bond Theory, Molecular Orbital Theory, and Hydrogen bonding.

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics
competency_groups.description: Application of thermodynamic laws to determine energy changes and reaction feasibility.
competencies: Define system, surroundings, state functions, and types of processes.
Calculate enthalpy changes (ΔH) using calorimetry data for various reactions.
Apply Gibbs Energy criteria to predict spontaneity and equilibrium conditions.
Relate entropy change with the second law of thermodynamics.

competency_groups.title: Equilibrium
competency_groups.description: Analysis of dynamic equilibrium in physical and chemical processes, including ionic equilibria.
competencies: Distinguish between static and dynamic equilibrium states.
Calculate equilibrium constants (K) from concentrations or partial pressures.
Apply Le Chatelier's principle to predict shifts due to concentration/pressure changes.
Analyze acid-base ionization, buffer solutions, and solubility product equilibria.

competency_groups.title: Redox Reactions
competency_groups.description: Proficiency in identifying oxidation-reduction processes and balancing redox equations.
competencies: Define oxidation and reduction via electron transfer mechanisms.
Assign oxidation numbers to elements in compounds using standard rules.
Balance complex redox reactions using ion-electron method or oxidation number method.
Relate redox processes to electrode potentials and electrochemical cells.

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques
competency_groups.description: Fundamental skills in organic nomenclature, isomerism, reaction mechanisms, and analysis.
competencies: Apply IUPAC rules for systematic naming of organic compounds.
Identify different types of structural and stereoisomers.
Classify functional groups and predict reactivity based on electronic effects (inductive/resonance).
Execute purification techniques and qualitative/quantitative analysis methods.
competency_groups.unit_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques
competency_groups.chapter_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbons
competency_groups.description: Understanding the structure, properties, and reactions of aliphatic and aromatic hydrocarbons.
competencies: Differentiate between saturated (alkanes), unsaturated (alkenes/alkynes), and aromatic compounds.
Predict products of addition, substitution, and elimination reactions in hydrocarbons.
Explain the stability and reactivity patterns of benzene rings.
Assess environmental impacts such as carcinogenicity associated with specific hydrocarbon classes.

competency_groups.title: Haloalkanes and Haloarenes
competency_groups.description: Analysis of halogenated compounds, their preparation methods, and reaction mechanisms.
competencies: Classify haloalkanes and haloarenes based on structure and nomenclature rules.
Explain the nature of C-X bond polarity and its effect on reactivity.
Predict products of nucleophilic substitution (SN1/SN2) and elimination reactions.
Discuss environmental implications of polyhalogen compounds.

competency_groups.title: Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers
competency_groups.description: Understanding the properties and reactions of oxygen-containing organic functional groups.
competencies: Distinguish between alcohols, phenols, and ethers based on structure and acidity.
Describe methods for preparation and commercial importance of key compounds (e.g., ethanol).
Predict reaction outcomes involving dehydration, oxidation, and ether cleavage.

competency_groups.title: Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids
competency_groups.description: Mastery of carbonyl chemistry including synthesis, properties, and reactions.
competencies: Differentiate aldehydes from ketones based on structure and reactivity.
Apply mechanisms for nucleophilic addition to carbonyl groups.
Describe oxidation/reduction pathways converting between functional group classes.
Explain the acidic nature of carboxylic acids and their derivatives.

competency_groups.title: Amines
competency_groups.description: Understanding nitrogen-containing organic compounds and their synthetic utility.
competencies: Classify amines as primary, secondary, or tertiary based on structure.
Explain basicity trends in aliphatic vs aromatic amines.
Describe the synthesis of diazonium salts and their role in azo dye formation.

competency_groups.title: Biomolecules
competency_groups.description: Knowledge of the structure and function of essential biological macromolecules.
competencies: Classify carbohydrates based on hydrolysis products (mono-, di-, polysaccharides).
Describe protein structures from primary to quaternary levels.
Identify roles of vitamins, hormones, and nucleic acids in biological systems.

competency_groups.title: Solutions
competency_groups.description: Quantitative analysis of mixtures and colligative properties.
competencies: Express concentration using various units (molarity, molality, mole fraction).
Apply Raoult's Law to ideal and non-ideal solutions.
Calculate molar mass from boiling point elevation or freezing point depression data.

competency_groups.title: Electrochemistry
competency_groups.description: Understanding electron transfer processes in galvanic and electrolytic cells.
competencies: Calculate cell potentials using the Nernst equation under non-standard conditions.
Relate conductivity to ion concentration and mobility in solutions.
Explain principles of batteries, fuel cells, and corrosion prevention.

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics
competency_groups.description: Analysis of reaction rates, factors influencing them, and theoretical models.
competencies: Determine rate laws and order of reactions from experimental data.
Apply integrated rate equations to calculate half-lives and concentrations over time.
Explain collision theory and the role of activation energy in temperature dependence.

competency_groups.title: d- and f-Block Elements
competency_groups.description: Understanding the properties, electronic configurations, and applications of transition metals.
competencies: Explain general trends in physical and chemical properties across d-block series.
Differentiate between lanthanoids and actinoids based on electronic structure.
Identify important compounds and their industrial or biological applications.

competency_groups.title: Coordination Compounds
competency_groups.description: Mastery of Werner's theory, nomenclature, bonding models, and isomerism.
competencies: Apply IUPAC rules for naming coordination compounds with complex ligands.
Distinguish between geometrical and optical isomers in coordination complexes.
Compare Valence Bond Theory and Crystal Field Theory regarding metal-ligand bonding.

unit_breakdown.topic: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

formative_topics: Surface Chemistry - Adsorption and Colloidal State
General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements

marks_distribution.component: Practical Examination for Visually Challenged Students (Classes XI & XII)

question_paper_design.rule_1: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
question_paper_design.rule_2: Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
question_paper_design.rule_3: There will be no overall choice in the question paper.

question_paper_design.internal_choice_policy: 33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.

competency_weightage.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

internal_assessment.policy: Schools can integrate formative topics with existing chapters as they align well.
formative_topics_class_xii: Surface Chemistry - Adsorption and Colloidal State
General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements

class_xi_mandatory_experiments_section_b: Quantitative analysis (Oxalic acid/KMnO4 or Mohr's salt)
Qualitative Analysis (One anion and one cation)

class_xii_mandatory_experiments_section_: Quantitative analysis (Oxalic acid/KMnO4 or Mohr's salt)
Qualitative Analysis (One anion and one cation)

class_xi_suggested_projects: Checking the bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
Study of the methods of purification of water

Testing the hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride, etc., depending upon the regional variation in drinking water and study of causes of presence of these ions above permissible limit (if any)
Investigation of the foaming capacity of different washing soaps and the effect of addition of Sodium carbonate on it
Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves
Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids
Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers
Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices
class_xii_suggested_projects: Study of the presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening
Study of quantity of casein present in different samples of milk
Preparation of soybean milk and its comparison with the natural milk with respect to curd formation, effect of temperature, etc.
Study of the effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions (temperature, concentration, time, etc.)
Study of digestion of starch by salivary amylase and effect of pH and temperature on it
Comparative study of the rate of fermentation of following materials: wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice, etc.
Extraction of essential oils present in Saunf (aniseed), Ajwain (carom), Illaichi (cardamom)
Study of common food adulterants in fat, oil, butter, sugar, turmeric power, chili powder and pepper

practicals.title: Comparing the pH of strong and weak acids
Study pH change in titration using universal indicator
Study the shift in equilibrium between ferric ions and thiocyanate ions
Study the shift in equilibrium between [Co(H2O)6]2+ and chloride ions
Preparation of standard solution of Oxalic acid/Sodium hydroxide/Hydrochloric acid
Determination of strength of Sodium hydroxide by titration against Oxalic acid
Determination of strength of Hydrochloric acid by titration against Sodium Carbonate
Qualitative Analysis (One anion and one cation)
practicals.details: Cations: Pb2+, Cu2+, Al3+, Fe3+, Mn2+, Ni2+, Zn2+, Co2+, Ca2+, Sr2+, Ba2+, Mg2+, NH4+. Anions: CO3 2-, S2-, SO3 2-, NO3 -, NO2 -, Cl-, Br-, I-, SO4 2-, PO4 3-, CH3COO-.

practicals.title: Detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds
Preparation of lyophilic and lyophobic sols (Starch/Egg albumin/Gum / Ferric hydroxide)
Dialysis of sol prepared in Surface Chemistry section
Study the role of emulsifying agents in stabilizing emulsion
Effect of concentration and temperature on rate of reaction (Sodium Thiosulphate + HCl)
Study of reaction rates (Iodide ion with Hydrogen Peroxide / KIO3 and Na2SO3 clock reaction)
Enthalpy of dissolution (Copper Sulphate or Potassium Nitrate) / Enthalpy of neutralization / Hydrogen bond formation study
Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn2+ || Cu2+/Cu with change in concentration
Separation of pigments by paper chromatography and determination of Rf values
Preparation of double salt (Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate or Potash Alum)
Preparation of Potassium Ferric Oxalate
Preparation of Organic Compound (Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, p-Nitroacetanilide, Aniline yellow)
Tests for functional groups (Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic, amino)
Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins in pure samples/foodstuffs
